organizations.role: Former longtime member and past chairman
organizations.notes: Perry Lorenz is a 50-year Austin resident who has worked primarily as a principal in the purchase, sale, development and management of downtown Austin real estate since 1977, forming and serving as managing general partner of numerous downtown development partnerships. With partners he has developed 1,000+ condominium units in mixed-use projects including Spring, BartonPlace, Este, The Pedernales, Saltillo Lofts and The Independent, and controlled full city blocks where several of downtown Austin's tallest towers now stand (including the site of the 1,022-ft Waterline). His Austin Chamber listing (Real Estate - Commercial, 1311-A E. Sixth St.) matches the East Austin office described in coverage of the Mitchell family. Zip 78703 (Old West Austin/Clarksville) is consistent. The filing's employer 'Mitchell Family Corp' is corroborated: Lorenz is the husband of Sheridan Mitchell Lorenz, daughter of the late Texas oil and gas billionaire George P. Mitchell, and father of Katherine Lorenz, president of the Cynthia and George Mitchell Foundation; Mitchell Family Corp is the family's Austin/The Woodlands family office. Note: Mitchell Family Corp is a family office and philanthropic-support entity, not an operating oil & gas company (Mitchell Energy & Development was sold to Devon Energy in 2002), so no oil_gas flag is asserted for Lorenz himself.

Confirmed as Jill Ramirez, President/CEO and co-founder of the Latino HealthCare Forum (LHCF, 6601 Felix Ave, Austin TX 78741) — the employer string 'LHCF' plus occupation 'CEO' match exactly. Holds master's degrees from UT Austin; former AISD bilingual speech pathologist and educational consultant; LHCF was the only federal ACA Navigator in Central Texas under her leadership. NOTE: the record's zip 79745 is Kermit, TX (Winkler County) and conflicts with the 'Austin, TX' location — almost certainly a data-entry error for an Austin 787xx zip; identity is nonetheless confirmed by the employer/occupation match. SEPARATE CAUTION: the record's fec_partisan_lean 0.49 / 87 federal donations should not be attributed to her without verification — 'Jill Ramirez' is a common name and FEC.gov and OpenSecrets both blocked direct retrieval, so the FEC PAC search was run via web search only and returned no PAC records tied to this individual. No Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration found. Also of public record: DOJ announced in 2020 that a federal grand jury in Austin indicted her on one count of conspiracy, one count of falsification of records with intent to obstruct, and one count of false statements, over a September 2015 agreement to pay 10% of the nonprofit's federal grant proceeds to Frank Rodriguez, LHCF's co-founder and then a senior policy advisor to a City of Austin official; DOJ said Rodriguez pleaded guilty. These are charges, not convictions, and the disposition of her case is not established by the sources reviewed — recorded here as an unresolved allegation, and no affiliation is derived from it.
